title: Trachyspermum calcicola
intro: Trachyspermum calcicola, also known as Ajwain or Carum, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been widely used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including treating digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Trachyspermum calcicola include thymol, carvone, limonene, and other volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Trachyspermum calcicola is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or oil, and is also used as a key ingredient in Ayurvedic and Unani formulations for its carminative and expectorant properties.
taxonomy: Trachyspermum calcicola is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is a member of the genus Trachyspermum, which includes several species of aromatic plants. The plant is native to India and is also known as Indian dill or dhania. The family Apiaceae is commonly known as the parsley or carrot family, which includes many edible and medicinal plants. The classification of Trachyspermum calcicola helps in understanding its characteristics and uses.
